title: URL Encoder/Decoder: Percent-Encode & Decode URLs Online
description: Ever wondered why simple links break when shared? Uncover the hidden mechanics of percent encoding to instantly fix any URL online.

@graph.headline: URL Encoder/Decoder: Percent-Encode & Decode URLs Online
@graph.description: Ever wondered why simple links break when shared? Uncover the hidden mechanics of percent encoding to instantly fix any URL online.

acceptedAnswer.text: A URL encoder translates characters into a format universally accepted by web browsers and servers. It replaces reserved or unsafe characters with a '%' followed by two hexadecimal digits, ensuring the URL is transmitted correctly.

mainEntity.name: How to URL decode a string online?

acceptedAnswer.text: To URL decode a string online, simply paste the encoded string (containing % signs and hex digits) into our free URL decode tool, select the 'Decode' option, and the original readable characters will be instantly restored.

mainEntity.name: What is percent encoding and why is it used?

acceptedAnswer.text: Percent encoding, also known as URL encoding, is a mechanism for encoding information in a Uniform Resource Identifier (URI). It is used to safely transmit data containing spaces, special characters, or non-ASCII characters over the web by converting them into valid ASCII formats.

mainEntity.name: How to URL encode online securely?

acceptedAnswer.text: You can URL encode online securely using our tool, which processes all data locally in your browser. No data is sent to external servers for encoding or decoding, ensuring your sensitive URLs and query parameters remain completely private.

mainEntity.name: What is the difference between encodeURI and encodeURIComponent?

acceptedAnswer.text: encodeURI is meant for full URLs and ignores protocol and domain delimiters like '://', '/', '?', and '&'. encodeURIComponent is designed for specific query parameters or path segments and encodes everything, including those delimiters, to prevent them from breaking the URL structure.
